stories.why_it_matters: Kohli's replacement will get a rare opportunity to cement an ODI spot in a relatively lower-pressure fixture — a strong performance could set up a longer run in the squad. For fans and selectors alike, the series is a live audition that could shape India's ODI plans for upcoming tournaments. The outcome also signals how India manage workload and succession for their senior stars.
stories.whats_next: Expect the chosen replacement's performance to be closely watched by selectors ahead of future ODI series. Rain's impact on the Dharamsala fixture could shorten playing time and limit how much can be assessed in the first match.

stories.headline: BCCI weighing bilateral series feasibility; IPL 2026 likely to begin from early March
stories.what: India Today reports that the BCCI is evaluating the feasibility of hosting bilateral cricket series, while IPL 2026 is likely to get underway from early March. The board's scheduling deliberations come against the backdrop of a packed international calendar and the need to carve out a window for the lucrative T20 league. An early March start for IPL 2026 would be one of its earliest-ever editions, reflecting the pressure of competing events in the global cricket calendar.
stories.lens: An early IPL start affects when fans get their favourite tournament and signals how Indian cricket will be scheduled around ICC events for the next year.
stories.what_happened: India Today reports that the BCCI is currently assessing the practicality of scheduling bilateral international series alongside its existing commitments. According to the publication, IPL 2026 is expected to begin from early March, which would mark an earlier start than many recent editions. The board is reportedly weighing the logistical and cricketing demands of accommodating both bilateral fixtures and the IPL window. This planning is taking place as India manages a busy international schedule that includes bilateral series with multiple nations.
stories.context: The IPL, now in its late teens as a tournament, is the world's richest T20 cricket league and the BCCI's largest revenue generator. In recent years, the IPL window has typically run from late March through May, though the precise dates shift annually based on the international calendar and venue availability. BCCI's scheduling decisions carry enormous consequences for broadcasters, franchises, sponsors, and players. Bilateral series with visiting nations are also significant revenue earners but must be balanced against player workloads and franchise interests.
stories.why_it_matters: An early March IPL start means fans get more cricket sooner, but it also compresses preparation time for franchises and players returning from international duty. For broadcasters and fantasy gaming platforms, the IPL start date is a major commercial trigger. Clarity on bilateral series also affects touring nations' planning and India's international commitments.
stories.whats_next: Expect the BCCI to formalise the IPL 2026 schedule and announce bilateral fixtures in the coming weeks. Franchise owners and team management will be watching for official confirmation so pre-season preparations and player auctions can be planned accordingly.

stories.headline: India women eye strong start against Pakistan in Women's T20 World Cup 2026
stories.what: Olympics.com reports that India Women will face arch-rivals Pakistan Women in the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026, with the Indian side looking to begin the tournament on a winning note. The India-Pakistan women's cricket contest carries all the intensity of its men's counterpart and is one of the most anticipated fixtures in women's cricket. India have a historically dominant head-to-head record against Pakistan in T20Is and will enter as firm favourites.
stories.lens: India vs Pakistan in any World Cup — even women's cricket — is appointment viewing for millions of Indian fans, and a strong start can set the tone for the entire tournament.
stories.what_happened: Olympics.com reports that India Women are preparing to face Pakistan Women in the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026, with the Indian team targeting a confident opening victory against their traditional rivals. The publication provides head-to-head data and broadcast details for the fixture, highlighting the significance of the match as a group-stage encounter that can shape both teams' tournament trajectories. India's women's team has grown into a formidable T20 side over recent years and enters the match as favourites based on current form and historical record.
stories.context: The India-Pakistan rivalry in women's cricket mirrors the intensity of the men's game, with political and sporting tensions making every meeting between the two sides significant. India Women have generally dominated Pakistan Women in T20 World Cup fixtures, rarely losing to them in tournament conditions. The ICC Women's T20 World Cup has grown substantially in profile and viewership, particularly in India following the women's team's improved performances in recent ICC events. Players like Smriti Mandhana and Deepti Sharma have been central to India's rise in women's T20 cricket.
stories.why_it_matters: A win for India Women over Pakistan in a World Cup fixture would energise the women's cricket fanbase and keep India in contention for the knockout stages. The match also matters commercially — India vs Pakistan fixtures attract premium viewership and sponsorship attention even in women's cricket, helping grow the game further.
stories.whats_next: Expect massive media coverage and social media engagement around the fixture. India's performance against Pakistan will be treated as an early indicator of their title credentials for the rest of the Women's T20 World Cup 2026.

stories.headline: Manav Suthar makes immediate impact in county cricket after India debut
stories.what: India Today reports that Manav Suthar, fresh from making his India debut, has quickly made his mark in county cricket as well. The young cricketer's seamless transition from an international debut to performing in county conditions reflects both his skill and adaptability. Suthar's rapid rise — from domestic cricket to the Indian squad and now performing overseas — signals the emergence of another promising talent in Indian cricket's pipeline.
stories.lens: Every time a young Indian cricketer succeeds abroad, it builds confidence that India's talent pool is deep — and Suthar's story is one fans and selectors will track closely.
stories.what_happened: India Today reports that Manav Suthar, who recently made his debut for the Indian national side, has wasted no time in making an impression in county cricket. The publication notes that Suthar has made an instant impact after arriving in the county circuit, suggesting strong early performances that caught attention. His ability to perform at multiple levels in quick succession — domestic cricket, India debut, and then county cricket — highlights his current form and confidence. The report underscores India's continued pipeline of young talent entering international and global circuits.
stories.context: County cricket in England is regarded as one of the most demanding domestic competitions in the world, with its swinging conditions and high-quality opposition providing a stern test for any cricketer. Indian players participating in county cricket has become increasingly common, with the BCCI allowing certain players to gain experience abroad ahead of or following international commitments. Manav Suthar is a young spin-bowling all-rounder from Rajasthan who impressed in domestic tournaments, earning his India call-up. His India debut marked a significant milestone in his career, and his county performance adds to the momentum.
stories.why_it_matters: Suthar's success in county cricket strengthens his case for a more regular spot in the Indian squad, as overseas experience in challenging conditions is highly valued by selectors. For Indian cricket fans, his emergence is a sign of the country's ever-deepening talent pool beyond the established stars. It also keeps the spotlight on young players who could be key figures in Indian cricket over the next decade.
stories.whats_next: Expect Suthar's county performances to be tracked by Indian selectors as evidence of his adaptability and temperament. A strong run in county cricket could translate into more international opportunities in the near future.

stories.headline: Zabaan Sambhal Ke actor Dinyar Tirandaz passes away
stories.what: Dinyar Tirandaz, best known for his role in the iconic Hindi sitcom Zabaan Sambhal Ke, has died. The show, which aired in the 1990s, was one of Doordarshan's most beloved comedies and made Tirandaz a familiar face in Indian households. His passing marks the loss of a veteran of Indian television's golden era. Details about the cause of death have not been specified in reports.
stories.lens: The death of a beloved face from Doordarshan's golden age of comedy is a moment of nostalgia and loss for an entire generation of Indian TV viewers.
stories.what_happened: The Indian Express reports that Dinyar Tirandaz, the actor widely recognised for his role in the popular Hindi sitcom Zabaan Sambhal Ke, has passed away. The publication broke the news without specifying the cause or exact circumstances of his death. Tirandaz was a prominent figure in Indian television, particularly associated with the Doordarshan era of the 1990s. His death has prompted an outpouring of condolences from the entertainment community and fans who grew up watching the show.
stories.context: Zabaan Sambhal Ke was a hugely popular Hindi sitcom that aired on Doordarshan in the 1990s, at a time when the state broadcaster was the dominant entertainment platform for Indian families. The show was known for its sharp, clean comedy and memorable ensemble cast, and it ran for multiple seasons, earning a devoted national following. Dinyar Tirandaz was a theatre-trained actor who brought considerable comic timing and character depth to his role. The 1990s Doordarshan era remains a touchstone of collective Indian cultural memory, with its shows continuing to be fondly referenced decades later.
stories.why_it_matters: For millions of Indians who grew up in the 1990s, Zabaan Sambhal Ke and its cast were a weekly ritual, making Tirandaz's death a genuinely felt cultural loss. The passing of veterans from this era serves as a reminder of the thinning ranks of Indian television's foundational generation of performers. His death will prompt renewed appreciation for the Doordarshan comedy legacy among older audiences.
stories.whats_next: Expect tributes from Bollywood and television fraternity members to continue emerging over the coming days. It is likely that retrospective coverage of Zabaan Sambhal Ke and Tirandaz's body of work will appear across entertainment platforms as fans revisit the show.

stories.headline: Shahid Kapoor overhauls script selection process after Deva and O Romeo fail at box office
stories.what: Shahid Kapoor is reportedly now insisting on taking film narrations alongside his core team following the back-to-back box-office failures of Deva and O Romeo. The actor appears to be recalibrating his professional approach to script selection after a difficult run. The change signals a more collaborative, committee-style decision-making process for one of Bollywood's prominent leading men. It reflects a wider industry trend of stars becoming more cautious after costly flops.
stories.lens: When a top Bollywood star publicly changes how he picks films after repeated failures, it reveals the enormous financial stakes involved and the pressure big-budget Hindi cinema faces from OTT competition.
stories.what_happened: The Indian Express reports that Shahid Kapoor is now insisting on attending script narrations with his team rather than evaluating projects alone, a shift driven by the poor box-office performance of his recent films Deva and O Romeo. The report suggests the actor is taking a more guarded and collective approach to future project choices. Both films underperformed commercially, making this course correction a direct response to professional setbacks. The publication presents this as a significant change in how Kapoor manages his career decision-making.
stories.context: Shahid Kapoor is one of Hindi cinema's established A-list actors, having delivered major hits including Kabir Singh and Jab We Met. However, even top stars have faced severe box-office pressure in the post-pandemic era as Hindi films increasingly struggle against South Indian dubbed releases and OTT platforms. Deva, an action thriller, and O Romeo both failed to find audiences, adding financial and critical disappointments to Kapoor's recent record. The practice of stars involving managers, producers, and trusted collaborators in script decisions is becoming more common in an industry where a single big-budget failure can cost tens to hundreds of crores.
stories.why_it_matters: Repeated failures by a bankable star like Shahid Kapoor directly affect the livelihoods of hundreds of crew members and the finances of producers and distributors who back his films. His recalibration signals the acute uncertainty gripping mainstream Bollywood, where even established names are struggling to guarantee returns. For audiences, it raises the possibility that Kapoor's future projects will be more carefully chosen and potentially more watchable.
stories.whats_next: Expect Shahid Kapoor's next project announcement to be watched closely for signs of this more cautious approach. It is likely that his team will publicly emphasise script quality and audience research before his next major release is greenlit.

stories.headline: Akshay Kumar dismisses Welcome To The Jungle salary rumours and addresses retirement
stories.what: Akshay Kumar has publicly denied rumours about his salary for the upcoming ensemble comedy Welcome To The Jungle and also spoke about his future plans regarding retirement from films. The star's salary has been a subject of industry gossip, prompting him to address it directly. His comments on retirement are noteworthy given his unusually prolific output and his recent string of box-office disappointments. Welcome To The Jungle is one of the most high-profile Bollywood releases in the pipeline.
stories.lens: Akshay Kumar addressing both his pay and retirement in one breath underlines the intense scrutiny Bollywood's highest-paid stars face as the industry grapples with a credibility and box-office crisis.
stories.what_happened: The Indian Express reports that Akshay Kumar has dismissed circulating rumours about his remuneration for Welcome To The Jungle, an upcoming large ensemble Bollywood comedy. In the same interaction, Kumar opened up about the question of retirement, offering his perspective on how long he intends to continue working in films. The actor did not confirm the rumoured salary figures, effectively denying their accuracy. The comments come at a time when Kumar's commercial standing has been questioned following several consecutive box-office failures.
stories.context: Akshay Kumar has historically been among the highest-paid actors in Bollywood and one of its most prolific, regularly releasing three to four films a year. However, a prolonged run of box-office underperformers — including films like Ram Setu, Selfie, and Bade Miyan Chote Miyan — has significantly dented his image as a reliable commercial draw. Welcome To The Jungle is a sequel in the Welcome franchise, which was a major hit in the late 2000s. Salary rumours around top stars are common in Bollywood and often relate to debates about whether their fees are justified given recent performance.
stories.why_it_matters: Akshay Kumar's commercial viability directly affects the investment decisions of producers and studios who back expensive Bollywood productions, with his films typically involving budgets of hundreds of crores. If top stars command high fees regardless of recent performance, it squeezes margins across the industry and filters down to smaller productions. His comments on retirement, however casual, will be closely parsed by fans, producers, and the trade.
stories.whats_next: Expect Welcome To The Jungle's release to be a major litmus test for whether Kumar can reclaim his box-office standing. It is likely that salary and budget details will continue to circulate as the film approaches its release date.

stories.headline: Nora Fatehi lands FIFA World Cup song as Indian stars push into global sports entertainment
stories.what: Nora Fatehi has secured a song for the FIFA World Cup, marking a significant crossover moment for an Indian entertainment personality into the world's biggest sporting event. The development is being analysed as part of a broader trend of Indian stars gaining visibility in global sports entertainment. FIFA World Cup songs are among the most-watched music videos globally, making this a high-profile platform. It underscores India's growing soft-power footprint in international popular culture.
stories.lens: An Indian star landing a FIFA World Cup song is a rare, concrete milestone that signals India's entertainment industry is no longer just a domestic giant but a genuine player in global pop culture.
stories.what_happened: News18 reports that Nora Fatehi has been associated with a song for the FIFA World Cup, framing it within a broader analysis of how Indian stars are entering global sports entertainment. The report examines the significance of this development in the context of India's expanding cultural influence internationally. Fatehi, who has a large global following particularly in North Africa and the Middle East, is described as a representative of this emerging trend. The story highlights that Indian talent is increasingly being sought for marquee global events.
stories.context: Nora Fatehi is a Canadian-Moroccan dancer and actress based in India who rose to prominence through Bollywood item numbers and reality TV judging. She has a substantial following across the Middle East and North Africa, giving her a cross-cultural appeal that is rare among India-based entertainers. The FIFA World Cup, scheduled to be held in the United States, Canada, and Mexico in 2026, generates some of the highest global viewership of any single event. Official World Cup songs have historically been global chart phenomena, with previous editions featuring artists like Shakira and Pitbull.
stories.why_it_matters: For Indian fans and the entertainment industry, Fatehi's association with the FIFA World Cup song is a point of national pride and a commercial opportunity that extends Bollywood's reach well beyond South Asia. It also demonstrates that India-based artists can compete for and win slots on the world's biggest entertainment stages. This kind of global visibility can open further international doors for Indian performers, directors, and music producers.
stories.whats_next: Expect the song's release to generate significant media coverage in India and internationally. It is likely that Fatehi's global bookings and brand endorsements will increase on the back of this association.

stories.headline: OTT this week: Ali Fazal's crime series Raakh and Jason Statham's Shelter arrive on streaming
stories.what: Two notable titles are landing on OTT platforms this week — Raakh, a crime series led by Indian actor Ali Fazal, and Shelter, a survival thriller starring Hollywood's Jason Statham. The pairing of a high-profile Indian original series with a major Hollywood release makes for one of the stronger streaming weekends in recent weeks. Ali Fazal, who has crossover credentials from the Victoria & Abdul franchise, headlining an Indian crime series adds particular weight to the local offering. Both titles cater to action and thriller audiences.
stories.lens: With a serious Ali Fazal-led Indian original and a Jason Statham Hollywood thriller dropping simultaneously, this is a strong streaming weekend that gives Indian OTT subscribers clear must-watch options.
stories.what_happened: The Indian Express recommends two OTT titles for the weekend: Raakh, an Indian crime series featuring Ali Fazal in the lead role, and Shelter, described as a survival thriller starring Jason Statham. The publication frames both as the standout streaming choices of the week across platforms. Raakh represents a significant Indian original production given Fazal's profile, while Shelter adds Hollywood star power to the weekend lineup. No specific platform names are confirmed from the headline alone, but both are presented as currently available for Indian viewers.
stories.context: Ali Fazal is one of the few Indian actors to have successfully built a profile in both Bollywood and international productions, appearing in the Hollywood hit Furious 7 and the critically acclaimed British film Victoria & Abdul alongside Dame Judi Dench. His return to a lead role in an Indian crime series is therefore a high-expectation project. Jason Statham is one of Hollywood's most consistent action stars, known for the Transporter and Mechanic franchises. The Indian OTT market has exploded in scale, with platforms like Netflix, Prime Video, JioCinema, and SonyLIV competing fiercely for subscriber attention with a mix of Indian originals and international acquisitions.
stories.why_it_matters: For Indian OTT subscribers, the simultaneous availability of a credible Indian original crime series and a mainstream Hollywood thriller represents strong value in a market where content quality is the primary driver of subscription decisions. Ali Fazal's involvement in Raakh could set a benchmark for Indian crime drama production values. The weekend lineup also illustrates how Indian and global content increasingly compete for the same viewer's attention on the same platforms.
stories.whats_next: Expect reviews and social media reactions for both Raakh and Shelter to drive or dampen viewership significantly within the first 48 hours of release. It is likely that Raakh's performance will influence commissioning decisions for future Indian crime originals on its platform.

stories.headline: Backrooms review: Kane Parsons' horror film earns praise for innovation
stories.what: The Backrooms, directed by Kane Parsons, has received a positive review from The Indian Express, which describes it as an innovative horror film that successfully reads the room in terms of audience expectations. Parsons, who rose to fame as a teenage YouTube filmmaker, is being celebrated for bringing fresh ideas to the horror genre. The film is based on the internet creepypasta mythology of the Backrooms, a liminal-space horror concept that became a viral phenomenon. Its critical reception marks a notable moment for internet-native filmmaking entering mainstream critical discourse.
stories.lens: A horror film born from internet culture getting serious critical attention is a signal of how online creativity is reshaping mainstream cinema — and what Indian horror filmmakers might learn from it.
stories.what_happened: The Indian Express has published a review of The Backrooms, directed by Kane Parsons, calling it an innovative horror film and praising its ability to meet audience expectations smartly. The review credits Parsons with bringing originality to the genre. The Backrooms is based on a widely known internet horror mythology involving endless, liminal office-like spaces. The film has been released and is available for Indian audiences to access, making the review timely guidance for viewers considering it.
stories.context: The Backrooms originated as an internet creepypasta — a piece of user-generated horror fiction — that described an unsettling, infinite maze of yellow-wallpapered rooms as a kind of psychological terror. The concept went massively viral on platforms like Reddit and YouTube, spawning countless fan films, the most notable of which were made by then-teenager Kane Parsons, whose short films accumulated millions of views. A24, the prestigious American independent film studio known for elevated horror, later backed Parsons to develop a full feature film, an extraordinary leap for a self-taught teenage filmmaker. The project attracted significant attention as an example of social-media talent being fast-tracked into professional Hollywood production.
stories.why_it_matters: For Indian horror fans and filmmakers, The Backrooms represents a case study in how internet-native storytelling can translate into commercially and critically viable cinema. A positive review from a major Indian publication will likely drive streaming viewership among the significant Indian horror audience. The film also raises interesting questions about whether Indian OTT platforms and studios might similarly scout and develop internet-native talent.
stories.whats_next: Expect The Backrooms to perform strongly on whichever streaming platform carries it in India following this critical endorsement. It is likely that Parsons' success will be cited as a model in Indian film industry conversations about discovering new directorial talent from online spaces.
